name: Popeye's Fried Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the first nine ingredients.
2. Place the cornflakes in another bowl.
3. Mix the eggs and cold water in a separate bowl.
4. Heat enough corn oil in a heavy, roomy skillet to be 1 inch deep.
5. Grease a 9x12x2 baking pan.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
7. Dip each chicken piece one at a time:
- First, into the dry coating mix.
- Second, into the egg and water mixture.
- Third, into the crushed cornflakes.
- Fourth, quickly but briefly back into the dry mix.
8. Drop the coated pieces into hot oil and fry, skin-side down, for 3 to 4 minutes on medium high heat.
9. Turn and fry the other side of each piece.
10. Do not crowd the pieces during frying.
11. Place the fried pieces in the prepared pan in a single layer, skin-side-up.
12. Cover the pan with foil, sealing it on three sides only, leaving one side loose for steam to escape.
13. Bake at 350°F for 35-40 minutes, then remove the foil and test for tenderness.
14. Allow to bake uncovered for an additional 5 minutes to crisp the coating.
15. Serves 4. Leftovers refrigerate well up to 4 days. Do not freeze leftovers. The leftover coating mix (first nine ingredients) can be stored at room temperature in a covered container up to 2 months.
